docs.metaDescription: The open AT Protocol record schemas behind GainForest — biodiversity observations, impact claims, funding, and identity.
docs.title: Data schemas
docs.lead: The open record formats behind GainForest. Every observation, project, certificate, donation, and post is stored as a structured record using the schemas below.

sections.feed: Social primitives ported from Bluesky into the GainForest commons — posts (and reply comments) and likes, so the feed can be posted to, commented on, and liked.
sections.dwc: Biodiversity occurrence records aligned with the TDWG Simple Darwin Core standard. Occurrences reference a shared event; measurements reference an occurrence.
sections.ac: Media and passive-acoustic-monitoring records aligned with TDWG Audiovisual Core. Audio and multimedia link to a Darwin Core occurrence as evidence.
sections.organization: Organization map-data records — published map layers (drone orthomosaics, GeoJSON overlays) and the named monitored areas that group repeat captures into change-over-time series.
sections.certified: Identity primitives from the Hypercerts ecosystem — actor profiles, organizations, follows, badges, and locations.
sections.hypercerts: Impact claims (activity, contribution, contributor, rights), collections, funding receipts, attachments, work scope, and shared types.

meta.title: Tainá — GainForest
meta.description: Connect your own Telegram bot and turn what you see in nature into verifiable biodiversity observations.

hero.title: Meet Tainá
hero.description: Tainá is your Telegram field companion. Send her a photo or a note about a plant, animal, or fungus — she identifies the species and records the observation under your GainForest account.
hero.step1Description: Create a private Telegram bot with @BotFather and link it here. Only you can talk to it.
hero.step2Description: Message your bot whatever you see in the field. Tainá identifies the species and asks for the details that matter.
hero.step3Description: Each confirmed sighting becomes a biodiversity observation saved under your account and visible on your profile.
hero.storageNote: Observations confirmed through Tainá are saved under your GainForest account. Photos and notes are stored with your account files.

signIn.title: Sign in to get started
signIn.description: Tainá records observations under your GainForest account, so sign in before connecting your bot.

setup.title: Connect your Tainá bot
setup.description: Create a Telegram bot for Tainá and paste its token below. The bot is private — it works only for you.
setup.howTitle: How to create your bot (about 2 minutes)
setup.howStep3: Copy the token it gives you (it looks like 123456789:ABC…).
setup.howStep4: Paste it below — this bot is used only by you.

errors.invalidToken: That doesn't look like a bot token. Copy it from @BotFather (it looks like 123456789:ABC…).
errors.genericError: We couldn't connect your bot. Please try again.
errors.runtimeUnreachable: Tainá is unreachable right now. Please try again in a moment.

activation.title: Activate your bot
activation.description: Your bot is connected but still private. Open it in Telegram and send this one-time code so only you can talk to it.
activation.hint: The button sends the code for you — or message the bot and type the code yourself.

linked.title: Your Tainá bot
linked.justLinkedNote: Your bot is linked and ready.
linked.description: You're running <bot></bot>. Open it in Telegram to log what you see, or review everything on your dashboard.

dashboard.loadError: We couldn't load your Tainá dashboard. Please try again.
dashboard.notLinkedDescription: You haven't connected a Telegram bot yet. Set one up and Tainá will start logging your observations here.
dashboard.activationDescription: <bot></bot> is connected but private. Open it in Telegram and send this one-time code so only you can talk to it.
dashboard.apiKeyDescription: A GainForest AI agent key created for Tainá. It also appears in Settings → AI agent keys, marked as Tainá's. Keep it secret.
dashboard.revokeConfirm: Revoke this key? Tainá will stop recording observations until you generate a new one.
dashboard.keyActionFailed: That didn't work — please try again.
dashboard.chatEmpty: No messages yet. Open your bot in Telegram and describe or photograph something you saw — your conversation with Tainá shows up here live.
dashboard.restartConfirm: Start a fresh conversation with Tainá? She'll forget this chat's history — your recorded observations are not affected.
dashboard.restartFailed: The restart didn't work — please try again.
dashboard.resetConfirm: Reset your Tainá agent? This disconnects your bot, deletes its chat history and your profile here, and revokes its key. Your recorded observations are not affected. You can set Tainá up again anytime.
dashboard.resetFailed: The reset didn’t work — please try again.
dashboard.profileDescription: A short Markdown profile stored with your agent so Tainá knows who you are. It shapes how she talks with you and the connections she can suggest.
dashboard.profilePromptIntro: Copy this prompt into ChatGPT or Claude to draft a USER.md profile, then send the Markdown it returns to your agent on Telegram — or paste it below.
dashboard.profilePromptBody:
Create a two-page, non-sensitive connection profile that helps people or agents find good reasons to talk with me, collaborate, trade notes, invite me into something, or introduce me to someone unexpected.

Don’t make it a resume, biography, personality type, or definitive account. Make it warm, specific, lightly imaginative, and useful for serendipity.

Draw out my recurring curiosities, live questions, tastes, projects, values-in-practice, conversation surfaces, possible offers/asks, and the kinds of people, scenes, collaborations, or opportunities I might resonate with. Include work, but don’t make work the whole frame.

Stay grounded: distinguish evidence from hunches, and phrase uncertain things as possibilities or questions. Don’t infer sensitive or private facts. Include what not to assume, and where more context would help.
dashboard.profilePlaceholder: Paste the Markdown profile here. Leave out secrets and anything you do not want stored with your agent.
dashboard.profileCounter: This becomes USER.md in your agent’s workspace. {count}/{max} characters.
dashboard.profileSaved: Saved — Tainá will use it from her next reply.
dashboard.profileSaveFailed: We couldn’t save your profile. Please try again.

meta.title: Submit field data
meta.description: Send large batches of field photos and survey exports to the GainForest team for review and publishing.

hero.title: Submit your field data in one big batch
hero.description: Pack your photos and survey exports (for example from KoboToolbox) into a single zip file and send it to us. Our team reviews every batch and publishes the observations to your account for you.
hero.step1Description: Photos, spreadsheets and survey exports — the format doesn't need to be perfect.
hero.step2Description: Files up to 10GB upload straight from your browser. Keep the page open until it finishes.
hero.step3Title: We review and publish for you
hero.step3Description: The GainForest team checks the data and publishes the observations to your account. You can follow the progress here.

signIn.title: Sign in to submit data
signIn.description: Batches are linked to your account so the observations end up in the right place.

form.consentHint: You can withdraw this permission at any time in Settings.
form.consentGranted: You've allowed the GainForest team to publish reviewed data to your account.
form.keepOpen: Keep this page open until the upload finishes.

errors.tooLarge: That file is too large — the limit is {max}.
errors.uploadFailed: The upload didn't complete. Check your connection and try again.

meta.title: AudioMoth — GainForest
meta.description: Set the clock, configure recording schedules and update firmware on your AudioMoth acoustic recorder, straight from the browser.

audiomoth.title: AudioMoth
audiomoth.subtitle: Plug an AudioMoth into your computer with a USB cable and switch it to USB/OFF. You can set its clock, change its recording settings and update its firmware right here — no extra apps needed.
audiomoth.unsupportedBrowser: This browser can't talk to USB devices. Please use a recent version of Chrome or Edge on a computer.

connection.noDeviceBody: Connect your AudioMoth via USB, set its switch to USB/OFF, then press the button to grant access.
connection.setupReasonSettings: Recording settings differ from the GainForest setup
connection.setupReasonSettingsUnknown: Recording settings haven't been applied from this computer yet

device.setTimeError: Couldn't set the clock. Check the connection and try again.

configure.scheduleBody: Choose up to four periods each day when the AudioMoth should record.
configure.noPeriodsHint: No recording periods — the device will not record.
configure.disable48DCFilterLabel: Turn off the 48 Hz low-frequency filter
configure.dailyFoldersLabel: Save recordings in a new folder each day
configure.filenameWithDeviceIdLabel: Include device ID in file names
configure.configureSuccess: Settings applied. The device clock was also set.
configure.configureError: The AudioMoth didn't confirm the new settings. Please try again.
configure.extendPrepTimeLabel: Extend WAV file preparation time for slow SD cards
configure.displayVoltageRangeLabel: Use NiMH/LiPo voltage range for battery level indication
configure.magneticSwitchLabel: Enable magnetic switch for delayed start
configure.chimeBody: Options for setting up the device with the AudioMoth mobile app's acoustic chime. Needs firmware 1.12.0 or newer.
configure.requireAcousticConfigLabel: Always require acoustic chime on switching to CUSTOM
configure.requireLocationInChimeLabel: Also require location in acoustic chime
configure.useTimeZoneInChimeLabel: Use time zone in acoustic chime
configure.adjustScheduleFromChimeLabel: Also adjust schedule to new time zone
configure.ignoreExternalMicLabel: Ignore external microphone for acoustic chime
configure.gpsBody: For AudioMoths with the GPS add-on. Needs firmware 1.11.0 or newer.
configure.gpsEnabledLabel: Enable GPS for time and location setting
configure.gpsFixModeLabel: Acquire GPS fix before and after

firmware.releasesError: Couldn't load the release list. You can still use a local file.
firmware.localFileLabel: Or use a firmware file from this computer (.bin)
firmware.invalidFirmwareFile: That file doesn't look like AudioMoth firmware.
firmware.clearSettingsLabel: Also clear the stored recording settings
firmware.noFirmwareSelected: Choose an official release or a local firmware file first.
firmware.manualBootloaderRequired: This AudioMoth can't switch to update mode automatically. Please use the desktop Flash App with the switch in USB/OFF while pressing the SWITCH button.
firmware.flashError: The update didn't finish. Detach and reattach your AudioMoth, then try again.
firmware.serialPickPrompt: The AudioMoth has switched to update mode and will reappear as a serial device. Press the button and pick it from the list.
firmware.serialUnsupported: This browser can't finish the update over serial. Please use Chrome or Edge on a computer.
firmware.flashSuccess: Firmware updated successfully (check code {crc}). The device is restarting — it may take a few seconds to reappear.

autoSetup.subtitle: Sit back — updating firmware, setting the clock, applying field-ready recording settings and saving the device to your equipment list.
autoSetup.firmwareManualSkip: This device needs a manual update — skipped. You can update it from the Firmware tab.
autoSetup.firmwareFailed: The firmware update didn't finish. Detach and reattach your AudioMoth, then try again.
autoSetup.clockSetting: Setting the clock from this computer…
autoSetup.clockFailed: Couldn't set the clock. Check the connection and try again.
autoSetup.settingsDone: 48 kHz, 60 s recordings with 4 min pauses, acoustic chime required
autoSetup.settingsFailed: The AudioMoth didn't confirm the settings. Please try again.
autoSetup.equipmentExists: Already in your equipment list as {name}
autoSetup.equipmentSaved: Saved to your equipment list as {name}
autoSetup.equipmentSignInSkip: Sign in to save this device to your equipment list — skipped.
autoSetup.equipmentFailed: Couldn't save the device to your equipment list.
autoSetup.equipmentUpdated: Updated {name} in your equipment list with the new setup details

deployments.intro: Deployments record where and when an AudioMoth was placed in the field. Each one plays an acoustic chime that sets the recorder's clock, location and deployment ID.
deployments.signInTitle: Sign in to see your deployments
deployments.signInBody: Your deployments are saved to your account. Sign in to create and manage them.
deployments.loadError: Something went wrong while loading your deployments. Please try again.
deployments.emptyBody: Create your first deployment to set up an AudioMoth in the field with the acoustic chime.
deployments.deleteFailed: The deployment could not be deleted. Please try again.
deployments.createIntro: Name the site, choose the location, then play the chime with this device's speaker held against the AudioMoth's microphone.
deployments.locationUnavailable: Your location could not be determined. Enter the coordinates by hand.
deployments.equipmentEmpty: No AudioMoths are registered in your equipment or your organizations' yet.
deployments.equipmentHint: Pick the registered AudioMoth you are deploying — including ones registered by your organizations — to link it to this event.
deployments.invalidId: The deployment ID must be 16 characters (0–9, a–f).
deployments.createFailed: The deployment could not be saved. Please try again.
deployments.playFailed: The chime could not be played. Please try again.
deployments.playing: Playing the chime — hold the speaker against the AudioMoth…
deployments.chimeHint: Set the AudioMoth's switch to CUSTOM and hold this device's speaker against it while the chime plays.
deployments.doneBody: The chime was played and the deployment was saved to your account. Play it again if the AudioMoth didn't catch it.
deployments.editIntro: Change the site name or the linked AudioMoth. The deployment ID, location and date are fixed to the chime that was already played.
deployments.updateFailed: The deployment could not be updated. Please try again.

upload.intro: Insert an AudioMoth SD card and choose it below. Recordings are recognised by the deployment ID embedded in each file, kept safe in archival storage and saved to your account.
upload.signInBody: Your recordings are saved to your account. Sign in to upload them.
upload.dropTitle: Choose your SD card or drop it here
upload.dropBody: Pick the SD card drive (or any folder of AudioMoth recordings). Files are read on this device first — nothing is uploaded until you confirm.
upload.noWavBody: No WAV files were found in the selection. Choose the top level of the AudioMoth SD card or a folder that contains recordings.
upload.unmatchedTitle: Deployment not found in your account
upload.noIdTitle: No deployment ID in these recordings
upload.unreadable: {count} files could not be read as WAV recordings and will be skipped.
upload.previewHint: Adds a small preview and a spectrogram of each recording to your account so it can be played and seen anywhere.
upload.doneBody: {count} recordings uploaded. {skipped} already existed and were skipped.
upload.notConfigured: Recording storage is not set up yet. Please contact the team.
upload.uploadFailed: The upload could not be completed. Please try again.
upload.deploymentFallback: Created automatically when uploading SD card recordings.
upload.readFailed: The SD card could not be read. Remove and re-insert it, then try again — or drag the folder onto this page instead.

recordings.title: Recordings
recordings.loadError: Recordings could not be loaded right now.
recordings.empty: No recordings uploaded from this deployment yet.
recordings.previewUnavailable: No preview available for this recording
recordings.accountEmptyBody: Recordings uploaded from an AudioMoth SD card will show up here, grouped by deployment.
recordings.explorerHint: Recorders capture around the clock — pick a day on the calendar, then tap an hour bar to narrow down.

metadata.title: Tainá devices — GainForest
metadata.description: Field updates from GainForest devices running Tainá, including whether each device is active and ready.
